shared_properties: Feedback popups are authored for transparent background compositing above combat actors and projectiles.
Core palette metadata remains primary #5C3317, background #2B1B17, and accent #D4AF37 while reward-state colors appear inside specs.colors_hex.
Typography stays bold and readable for brief one-second appearances.
differences: The sheet contains Perfect, Good, Combo, Damage, Heal, and Shield popup variants for common duel outcomes.
Perfect emphasizes cyan-white energy, Good uses lime, Combo uses gold, Damage uses crimson, Heal uses emerald, and Shield uses pale gold.
Each popup uses a distinct silhouette plate so feedback remains readable even when numeric text is layered separately.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the duel loop, projectile attributes, UI layout, and Bevy runtime constraints for Reflect Academy.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the warm academy hall mood, layered duel composition, and luminous spell contrast.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load combo_damage_popup_sheet.png during Loading and build a 3x2 atlas for transient combat feedback widgets.
runtime_notes: Play popup motion through transform tweening or UI translation while the authored sprite remains static.
Keep the atlas order deterministic so combat code can map result states directly to frames.
Fade alpha over time rather than scaling down the art aggressively so outline legibility remains intact.

context.usage: Short-lived popup plates shown near impact points or combo counters after duel results resolve.

layout_notes: Each popup frame reserves a centered 128x28 px text zone.
Outer glow stays inside the 180x64 frame bounds.
No frame should exceed 70% opaque coverage so layered numbers remain legible.

acceptance_criteria: Atlas canvas is exactly 540x128 px for six 180x64 frames with no frame gutter.
All popup frames remain legible against both dark backdrop and bright projectile effects.
Perfect, Good, Combo, Damage, Heal, and Shield frames remain distinguishable by color and silhouette.
